subject: math
instructions: Warm up with the first problems, then fly into the dragon word problems. Show your work, and if one is tricky, try a smaller step first. You've got this!

questions.prompt: Dragon problem: A hungry dragon eats 2 1/2 buckets of fish every day. How many buckets does it eat in 4 days?
Dragon problem: Two young dragons race across the bay. One flies 3.7 miles and the other flies 4.25 miles. How much farther did the second dragon fly?
Dragon problem: A trainer has 3/4 of an hour to practice and splits it equally among 3 dragons. How much time does each dragon get? (Give your answer in minutes.)
Dragon problem (two steps): The academy catches 48 fish. They give 1/3 of them to the baby dragons, then split the rest equally between 2 big dragons. How many fish does each big dragon get?
